description.en: Object yielded by scandir() to expose the file path and other file attributes of a directory entry. scandir() will provide as much of this information as possible without making additional system calls. When a stat() or lstat() system call is made, the os.DirEntry object will cache the result. os.DirEntry instances are not intended to be stored in long-lived data structures; if you know the file metadata has changed or if a long time has elapsed since calling scandir(), call os.stat(entry.pat...

description.en: The default search path used by exec*p* and spawn*p* if the environment doesn’t have a 'PATH' key. Also available via os.path.

excerpt.es: The default search path used by exec*p* and spawn*p* if the environment doesn’t have a 'PATH' key. Also available via os.path.
excerpt.en: The default search path used by exec*p* and spawn*p* if the environment doesn’t have a 'PATH' key. Also available via os.path.

description.en: The file path of the null device. For example: '/dev/null' for POSIX, 'nul' for Windows. Also available via os.path.

excerpt.es: The file path of the null device. For example: '/dev/null' for POSIX, 'nul' for Windows. Also available via os.path.
excerpt.en: The file path of the null device. For example: '/dev/null' for POSIX, 'nul' for Windows. Also available via os.path.

description.en: Return a string describing the encoding of the device associated with fd if it is connected to a terminal; else return None. On Unix, if the Python UTF-8 Mode is enabled, return 'UTF-8' rather than the device encoding. Changed in version 3.10: On Unix, the function now implements the Python UTF-8 Mode.

description.en: Return a duplicate of file descriptor fd. The new file descriptor is non-inheritable. On Windows, when duplicating a standard stream (0: stdin, 1: stdout, 2: stderr), the new file descriptor is inheritable. Availability: not WASI. Changed in version 3.4: The new file descriptor is now non-inheritable.

description.en: Duplicate file descriptor fd to fd2, closing the latter first if necessary. Return fd2. The new file descriptor is inheritable by default or non-inheritable if inheritable is False. Availability: not WASI. Changed in version 3.4: Add the optional inheritable parameter. Changed in version 3.7: Return fd2 on success. Previously, None was always returned.
